experiment: Forcing profile and critical strip barrier mapping
description: Tier 1 experiments mapping the sedenion forcing pressure at non-trivial zeros and tracking structural stability (ZDTP convergence) across the sigma=1.0 analytic barrier.

forcing_profile.description: Commutator norm ||[u_antisym, F_base(t)]|| at first 5 Riemann zeros. Measures forcing pressure pushing zeros toward sigma=1/2.

zdtp_sigma_sweep.description: ZDTP convergence sweep for Euler product factors across the sigma=1.0 boundary at t=gamma_1.

experiment_2_pattern_probe.description: Conjugation symmetry tracking for F(t, sigma) moving from critical line (0.5) to boundary (1.0).

interpretation: Forcing pressure remains consistently high across the first 5 zeros, supporting the universal forcing hypothesis. ZDTP convergence identifies sigma=1.0 as a structural transition point where stability begins to degrade upon entering the critical strip. The dip at sigma=0.3 suggests a secondary transition or resonance near the strip boundary.
phase_70_relevance: Empirical grounding for Route 1 (Analytic Continuation) and Route 3 (Forcing Arguments). Maps the 'pressure gradient' that bilateral_collapse_continuation formalizes.
